title: Tempting Crunchy Australian Almond Tart
description: A rich and crunchy Australian dessert featuring marzipan, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this tart is shojin-friendly and made with pressure-cooked ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the almond flour, marzipan, and salt.
Add the coconut oil, maple syrup, and vanilla extract to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
Press the dough into the corners and up the sides of the pan.
Prick the bottom of the crust with a fork to prevent it from bubbling.
Line the crust with parchment paper and fill with pie weights or dried beans.
Bake the crust for 15 minutes, then remove the parchment paper and pie weights or beans.
Spread the sliced almonds over the bottom of the crust.
Pressure-cook the water in a pressure cooker for 5 minutes, then let the steam release naturally.
Strain the water and brush it over the almonds to make them crunchy.
Bake the tart for an additional 10 minutes, or until the almonds are golden brown.
